proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the binary features, at least one binary indicator (likely a treatment or comorbidity flag) is associated with a higher rate of objective_response in the cohort.
Among the continuous features, at least one (likely a disease-burden marker such as bone-marrow blast %, peripheral blast %, or WBC) is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.

analyses.code: for each col c: chi2_contingency for binary, ttest_ind by response for continuous
analyses.result_summary: Univariate screen of all 35 features against objective_response (overall ORR=0.1875). Strongest positive binary effects: feature_014 (+0.067 absolute, p=5.8e-70), feature_001 (+0.056, p=1.9e-55), feature_021 (+0.054, p=6.6e-51). Strongest negative effect: feature_004 ordinal 0/1/2 (-0.102 per level, p=1.8e-90). Strongest negative continuous: feature_020 (-0.51 unit diff between responders/nonresponders, p=1.7e-33), feature_024 (-1.80, p=2.0e-16), feature_022 (-1.11 on log scale, p=1.5e-4), feature_027 (-0.48 on log scale, p=1.6e-6).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_004 (ordinal 0/1/2; behaves like an ECOG performance-status proxy) is independently associated with lower objective_response after controlling for all other features.
feature_001, feature_014, and feature_021 each independently increase the probability of objective_response after multivariable adjustment, consistent with three separate treatment indicators.
feature_026 and feature_009 are independently associated with lower objective_response after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(objective_response ~ all 35 features, with log1p transforms for skewed continuous features).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logistic regression. feature_004 OR per level = exp(-0.362)=0.70 (p<1e-90). feature_001 OR=exp(0.374)=1.45 (p<1e-50), feature_014 OR=exp(0.436)=1.55 (p<1e-70), feature_021 OR=exp(0.361)=1.43 (p<1e-50). feature_026 OR=exp(-0.169)=0.84 (p=5.7e-7), feature_009 OR=exp(-0.188)=0.83 (p=3.8e-6). Other independently significant negative predictors: log(feature_022) WBC, feature_024 BM blast %, feature_020 PB blasts, log(feature_027). Independently significant positive predictor: feature_029.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001, feature_014, and feature_021 are not mutually exclusive: each pair co-occurs at the rate expected under independence, consistent with three separate co-administered treatment components.

analyses.code: pd.crosstab for each pair of (feature_001,feature_014,feature_021)
analyses.result_summary: All three pairwise cross-tabulations show approximately independent co-occurrence: e.g., P(f001=1)=0.401, P(f014=1)=0.300, joint P(f001=1,f014=1)=0.120 vs expected 0.120; P(f001=1,f021=1)=0.160 vs expected 0.161; P(f014=1,f021=1)=0.120 vs expected 0.120. No mutual exclusivity. Consistent with three independently assigned treatment flags.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving any single one of {feature_001, feature_014, feature_021} have a higher response rate than patients receiving none of them.
Patients receiving any two of {feature_001, feature_014, feature_021} have a higher response rate than patients receiving only one.

analyses.code: df['num_treat']=f001+f014+f021; df.groupby('num_treat')['objective_response'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Response rate by number of these treatments: 0=>0.172, 1=>0.162, 2=>0.163, 3=>0.633. Single and double combinations do NOT improve response over zero — they are essentially indistinguishable from baseline. Only the triple combination produces a dramatic uplift. Refutes h4.1 and h4.2.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving ALL three of feature_001, feature_014, and feature_021 simultaneously (the 'triple combination') have a substantially higher objective_response rate than patients receiving fewer than three.

analyses.code: df['triple']=(f001==1)&(f014==1)&(f021==1); ct=pd.crosstab(df['triple'],df['objective_response']); chi2_contingency(ct)
analyses.result_summary: Triple-positive subgroup (n=2401, 4.8% of cohort) has objective_response rate 0.633 vs 0.165 in all others (n=47599). Absolute risk difference +0.468. Chi-square p<<1e-300. Strongly supports h5.1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The synergistic effect is captured by a 3-way interaction term f001*f014*f021: the coefficient on the triple-product is significantly positive and large after fitting all main effects and 2-way interactions among the three.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(objective_response ~ t1+t2+t3+t1*t2+t1*t3+t2*t3+t1*t2*t3).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Saturated 2x2x2 model on the three treatments. Main and 2-way coefficients are all small or near zero (|coef|<0.11). The 3-way interaction t1*t2*t3 = +2.126 (p=2.6e-92). Confirms the effect is a pure 3-way synergy, not pairwise additive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within triple-treated patients only (n=2401), there exists at least one binary feature that strongly modifies (reduces) the response rate, identifying a resistance-marker subgroup.

analyses.code: for c in features: stratify trip[trip['triple']==1] by c, test response rate difference
analyses.result_summary: Two binary features dramatically reduce response within the triple-treated subgroup: feature_026 (15% prevalence within triple) drops response from 0.717 (f026=0) to 0.184 (f026=1), p=7.6e-86. feature_009 (12% prevalence) drops response from 0.691 (f009=0) to 0.188 (f009=1), p=3.3e-59. No other binary or continuous feature shows comparable modifier effect within this subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among triple-treated patients, the joint effect of feature_026=1 and feature_009=1 is approximately additive on the log-odds scale: each independently abolishes the synergistic treatment effect.
Triple-treated patients with neither feature_026 nor feature_009 (the 'clean triple' subgroup) have an objective_response rate near 0.78.

analyses.code: trip.groupby(['feature_026','feature_009'])['objective_response'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Within triple-treated (n=2401): f026=0,f009=0 -> 0.786 (n=1790); f026=0,f009=1 -> 0.187 (n=235); f026=1,f009=0 -> 0.182 (n=335); f026=1,f009=1 -> 0.195 (n=41). Either marker alone reduces response to baseline; both together no further reduction (consistent with each independently knocking out the effect). Strongly supports h8.1 and h8.2.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_026 has no effect on response in patients who do NOT receive the triple combination (i.e., it is a treatment-effect modifier, not an independent prognostic factor).
feature_009 has no effect on response in patients who do NOT receive the triple combination.

analyses.code: no_trip = df[df['triple']==0]; no_trip.groupby(['feature_026','feature_009'])['objective_response'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: In non-triple patients (n=47599): f026=0,f009=0 ORR 0.165; f026=0,f009=1 ORR 0.165; f026=1,f009=0 ORR 0.168; f026=1,f009=1 ORR 0.158. No effect. Confirms feature_026 and feature_009 act purely as triple-treatment effect modifiers (negative resistance markers), not main-effect prognostic factors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A formal logistic interaction model triple + f026 + f009 + triple*f026 + triple*f009 will show large negative coefficients on both interaction terms (each roughly equal in magnitude to the triple main effect), and small/zero coefficients on f026 and f009 alone.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y ~ triple + f026 + f009 + triple*f026 + triple*f009).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Coefficients (95% CI): triple +2.901 (2.79,3.02); f026 +0.019 (-0.05,0.09) NS; f009 -0.005 (-0.09,0.08) NS; triple*f026 -2.660 (-2.95,-2.37) p=4e-70; triple*f009 -2.550 (-2.89,-2.21) p=5e-50. Both interaction terms nearly cancel the triple main effect, confirming f026 and f009 each independently abolish the triple synergy.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Beyond feature_026 and feature_009, no other binary or continuous feature meaningfully modifies the triple-combination treatment effect (treatment-by-feature interaction screen).

analyses.code: for each c: sm.Logit(y ~ triple + c + triple*c).fit() and record p-value of triple*c
analyses.result_summary: Systematic screen of triple-by-feature interactions across all 32 non-treatment features. Top hits by p-value: feature_026 p=1.8e-62 (coef -2.44), feature_009 p=4.3e-42 (coef -2.26), feature_024 p=5.6e-4 (coef +0.15, modest), and all others p>0.08. Confirms feature_026 and feature_009 are the dominant modifiers; feature_024 (BM-blast %) shows a weak positive interaction (slightly larger triple effect at higher BM blasts).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The 'clean triple' subgroup defined as feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0 has objective_response rate ~0.79 vs ~0.16 in everyone else, with odds ratio > 15.

analyses.code: df['clean_triple'] = triple*(1-f026)*(1-f009); pd.crosstab(df['clean_triple'], y); odds ratio
analyses.result_summary: Clean-triple subgroup n=1790 (3.6% of cohort), ORR=0.786. All others n=48210, ORR=0.165. Absolute difference +0.621. Odds ratio = 18.6 (95% CI roughly 16.7-20.7). Chi-square p<<1e-300. Strongly supports h12.1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_004 (ordinal 0/1/2, performance-status-like) is associated with lower objective_response both within the clean-triple subgroup and overall — i.e., it is prognostic, not a treatment modifier.

analyses.code: ct = df[df['clean_triple']==1]; ct.groupby('feature_004')['objective_response'].mean(); also test triple*f004 interaction
analyses.result_summary: Within clean-triple: response rate 0.836 at f004=0, 0.778 at f004=1, 0.645 at f004=2 (linear-trend p=4.3e-10). Overall and within non-triple, similar gradient. Triple*feature_004 interaction p=0.34 (not significant). Confirms feature_004 is a main-effect prognostic factor, not a triple-effect modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_020 (peripheral blast count, mean 3.85, range 0-23.6) is negatively associated with objective_response across the cohort and within subgroups, with no treatment-modifier effect.

analyses.code: ttest by response; sm.Logit(y ~ triple + f020 + triple*f020).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Mean f020 = 3.95 in non-responders vs 3.44 in responders (p=1.7e-33). Within clean-triple: responders 1.46 vs nonresponders 2.25 (p=8e-4). Triple*f020 interaction p=0.24 (NS). Prognostic only.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_024 (continuous, range 20-100; behaves like bone-marrow blast %) is negatively associated with objective_response.
feature_022 (highly skewed continuous, range 0.5-500; behaves like WBC count) is negatively associated with objective_response on a log scale.

analyses.code: ttest by response; multivariable logit with log(f022) and f024
analyses.result_summary: feature_024 mean 60.3 in non-responders vs 58.5 in responders, t-test p=2.0e-16; in adjusted model OR=exp(-0.0052)=0.995 per unit (p=4e-18). feature_022 (log) coefficient -0.072 in adjusted model (p=3.2e-8). Both confirm classic disease-burden prognostic patterns.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_029 (continuous, range 1.7-5.5, mean 3.8; albumin-like) is positively associated with objective_response, both univariately and in adjusted models.

analyses.code: ttest by response; sm.Logit
analyses.result_summary: Mean f029: 3.79 in non-responders vs 3.82 in responders (p=2.7e-7). Adjusted logistic OR per 1-unit increase = exp(0.122)=1.13 (p=1.5e-7). Direction consistent with a positive prognostic biomarker (e.g., albumin).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 (highly skewed continuous, range 0.03-300, mean 6.1; bilirubin-like) is negatively associated with objective_response on a log scale.

analyses.code: ttest on log; sm.Logit
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted logistic coefficient on log(1+f027) = -0.080 per log-unit (p=1.4e-7). Direction supports it as a negative prognostic marker (consistent with hyperbilirubinemia or a similar liver-function abnormality).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 (continuous, range 18-92, mean 68; age-like) and feature_010 (binary, prevalence 0.45; sex-like) have NO independent effect on objective_response after adjustment.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y ~ all features).fit() — read coefficients on f011 and f010
analyses.result_summary: feature_011 adjusted coefficient -0.0004 per year (p=0.68); feature_010 coefficient +0.022 (p=0.35). Neither has a meaningful effect on response. Refutes any independent age or sex effect in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Most of the lower-prevalence binary indicators (feature_005, 006, 008, 015, 016, 018, 019, 025, 030, 032) have no independent effect on objective_response after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.code: Read coefficients on these features from the full multivariable logit
analyses.result_summary: All listed features have adjusted p-values > 0.12 with small coefficients (|beta|<0.06) in the full multivariable logit. None independently predicts response. Likely background variables (comorbidities, demographic flags, or weakly informative biomarkers).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the clean-triple subgroup, response can still be further refined: patients with feature_004=0 AND low feature_020 AND no f026/f009 will have the highest response rate.

analyses.code: df_ct = df[df['clean_triple']==1]; subset by f004==0 & f020<=1; .objective_response.mean()
analyses.result_summary: Clean-triple AND feature_004=0 AND feature_020<=1: n=394, ORR=0.879. Clean-triple overall ORR=0.786. Modest further enrichment (+0.09) but with smaller n. Supports h20.1 directionally; the additional refiners are prognostic factors that operate within and outside the treatment subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Reducing the triple-treatment subgroup definition by relaxing either of the two resistance markers (allowing f026=1 OR f009=1) substantially weakens the treatment effect.

analyses.code: Compute ORR for triple-treated stratified by allowing f026=1 vs allowing f009=1
analyses.result_summary: Triple-treated allowing f026=1 (any f009): ORR=0.633 overall vs 0.183 in f026=1 stratum vs 0.717 in f026=0. Allowing f009=1 similarly drops to 0.188 in that stratum. Both predicates (f026=0 AND f009=0) are required to preserve the high response rate. Confirms h21.1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among non-triple-treated patients (n=47599), no subgroup defined by features in this dataset achieves an objective_response rate above ~0.25 — i.e., there is no alternative effective treatment subgroup.

analyses.code: no_trip = df[df['triple']==0]; for each binary feature: groupby and test ORR; for continuous: split at terciles
analyses.result_summary: Across all single binary features and tercile splits of continuous features within non-triple patients, the highest subgroup ORR observed is ~0.20 (in f004=0, ORR=0.214; in low-blast strata, similar). No combination produces a response rate approaching that of clean-triple. Supports h22.1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A parsimonious adjusted model containing only clean_triple plus the principal prognostic features (f004, log(f022), f024, f020, f029, log(f027)) captures the main systematic signal in the data.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y ~ clean_triple + f004 + log(f022) + f024/100 + f020 + f029 + log(f027)).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Pseudo-R^2 = 0.082. Clean_triple coefficient = +3.001 (OR ~20.1, p<1e-300); feature_004 -0.392 (OR 0.68); log(f022) -0.080; f024 -0.562 per 100% (OR 0.57); f020 -0.040 per unit; f029 +0.130 per unit; log(f027) -0.080 per log-unit. All p<1e-5. Captures the main signal; remaining features add little.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The clean-triple effect is robust across strata of all major prognostic factors: it remains large (>+0.40 absolute) within strata of feature_004 (0,1,2), within high vs low feature_024, and within high vs low age (feature_011).

analyses.code: for each stratifier: compute ORR(clean_triple=1) - ORR(clean_triple=0) within each stratum
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_004=0: clean_triple ORR 0.836 vs 0.214 in others (Δ=+0.62). Within feature_004=2 (worst PS): 0.645 vs 0.080 (Δ=+0.57). Within feature_024 above median: ORR 0.78 vs 0.15 (Δ=+0.63). Within feature_011>=70 (older patients): 0.78 vs 0.15 (Δ=+0.63). Effect is highly consistent across strata, confirming the subgroup definition is robust.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS: Among patients with feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0, receiving the triple combination feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 increases objective_response from approximately 0.165 to approximately 0.786 (absolute risk difference +0.62, OR ~18-20). Both resistance-marker predicates (feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0) are required: presence of either marker abolishes the treatment effect entirely. None of the three component drugs alone (or in any pair) produces any detectable improvement.

analyses.code: Confirmatory: pd.crosstab(df['clean_triple'], df['objective_response']); odds ratio; chi-square; permutation invariance to stratifiers
analyses.result_summary: FINAL: Clean-triple subgroup (n=1790, 3.6% of cohort): ORR 0.786. Comparator (n=48210): ORR 0.165. Absolute risk difference +0.621. Odds ratio 18.6 (95% CI ~16.7-20.7). Chi-square p<<1e-300. The complete subgroup definition is: (feature_001=1) AND (feature_014=1) AND (feature_021=1) AND (feature_026=0) AND (feature_009=0). The two resistance markers (feature_026 and feature_009) have no independent effect on response in non-triple patients — they act purely as triple-treatment effect modifiers.
